name: Abe no Seimei
description: Abe no Seimei is a legendary figure in Japanese folklore, renowned as an onmyōji, a practitioner of onmyōdō, a traditional Japanese esoteric cosmology. He is believed to have lived during the Heian period (794–1185 AD) and is credited with extraordinary powers of divination and protection against evil spirits. Seimei's reputation grew significantly after his death, and he became a prominent figure in literature, kabuki theater, and popular culture. He is often depicted as a wise and powerful sorcerer, capable of manipulating the elements and communicating with supernatural entities. His legacy is deeply interwoven with Japanese occult traditions, and he is venerated as a patron saint of astrologers, diviners, and those seeking protection from malevolent forces. The Seimei Shrine in Kyoto commemorates his life and continues to be a site of pilgrimage and spiritual significance. His enduring popularity speaks to the persistent fascination with magic and the supernatural in Japanese culture.
subtitle: Master Onmyōji

keyMyths.title: Seimei's Birth and Supernatural Abilities
keyMyths.description: According to legend, Abe no Seimei was born with an innate understanding of onmyōdō due to his mother being a fox spirit, Kuzunoha. This imbued him with extraordinary magical abilities from a young age, allowing him to perceive and manipulate the spiritual realm with ease. His unparalleled skill in divination and exorcism soon brought him to the attention of the Imperial Court, where he served as a trusted advisor.
keyMyths.source: Historical and Literary Creations of Abe no Seimei by Haruo Sato

keyMyths.title: The Shikigami Incident
keyMyths.description: One popular myth recounts an incident where Seimei commanded shikigami, spirits bound to his service, to perform tasks. However, his wife, unaware of their presence, became frightened by them. To prevent future occurrences, Seimei concealed the shikigami within a cupboard, demonstrating his control over supernatural forces and his consideration for the mundane world. This tale illustrates the delicate balance between the magical and the ordinary in Seimei's life.
keyMyths.source: Onmyoji: A Novel by Baku Yumemakura

keyMyths.title: Seimei and the Emperor's Illness
keyMyths.description: The Emperor fell ill, and the court physicians were unable to cure him. Desperate, the Emperor summoned Abe no Seimei, who quickly diagnosed the illness as a curse placed by a vengeful spirit. Seimei performed a complex ritual, utilizing his knowledge of onmyōdō and his powerful shikigami to dispel the curse and restore the Emperor's health. This established Seimei's reputation as the most powerful onmyōji in the land.
keyMyths.source: Abe no Seimei: The History and Legend by Kazuo Yamada

sources.author: Haruo Sato
sources.work: Historical and Literary Creations of Abe no Seimei
sources.description: Examines the historical and literary portrayals of Abe no Seimei, separating fact from fiction.
sources.text: Examines the historical and literary portrayals of Abe no Seimei, separating fact from fiction.

sources.author: Baku Yumemakura
sources.description: A popular novel series that has greatly contributed to the modern image of Abe no Seimei, though not entirely historically accurate.
